page: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9354P processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.25 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 960 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in several memory and processing metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth, both read and write, rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Passmark memory scores and database operations also achieve top-tier status, reflecting the efficiency of the 5 GHz DDR5 memory subsystem. Multi-core stress-ng performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations fall into the top 25%, indicating strong parallel processing capabilities. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds with a small 135M model and top-tier text generation speeds with a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers high resource density by combining dedicated EPYC processing cores with a substantial 768.0 GB memory footprint. The primary tradeoff is the relatively modest local SSD storage capacity of 960 GB, which may require external storage solutions for data-heavy deployments. Given the top-tier database operation benchmarks and high memory bandwidth, this configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, medium-scale LLM inference, and data-dense enterprise applications.
description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9354P processor with 32 physical cores, 64 vCPUs, and a 3.25 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory and a 960 GB local SSD, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data positions this server in the top 10% for single-core CPU performance, large-block memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside top-25% multi-core and cryptography performance. The high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core makes it highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, though local storage is limited to 960 GB. This server is suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a 32-core AMD EPYC CPU, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD. It delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare metal server with 64 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs with 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9354P processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs
768 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 12 GB per core
Top-tier large-block memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks
960 GB local SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address
Suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and medium LLM inference
